mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5 percent within a 30-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding signals robust aggregate demand and household balance sheet health, which sustains earnings growth and lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and rising RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side capacity utilization is rising, which compresses input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO momentum relative to SPX 20-day returns drops below 0.02 during late-cycle periods, or forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month MA.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently above 2.5 percent annualized rate signals a healthy economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not statistically positive when GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 1.5 percent, the cost of capital increases sufficiently to pressure high-multiple growth stocks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 55 percent of instances following a FEDFUNDS cross above 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, which reduces wage-driven inflation fears and supports equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer credit outstanding growth decelerating below 3 percent year-over-year signals weakening household demand, which compresses near-term earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations when RSAFS growth decelerates below 3 percent YoY.
